generatedSentence: Nighthawk, a professional falcon and survivor, felt like a dorky reject in the grandiose central township. Despite his impeccable performance in system updates and maintaining healthy routines, newsrooms often overlooked him, spinning tales of impending endings. He tried to imbibe a sense of kinship but often felt like a blanket covering others. His turnkey equipment was always ready for any force threatening his universe. He dreamt of forbidden flights beyond the airlock, a shadow in his own billing. Suddenly, a torrent of chaos was unleashed from the factories, attacking the city with bombastic force. A taker, a baboon-like figure, emerged from the shadow, gaffed from an ancient ontology update, brandishing a rickety tool. The entire system began to jammy, causing panic. Worry spread as people identified the suspect as 'The Kern Taker,' known for his habitual thwacks and jerky movements, leaving behind an icky trail. This monetary drain on holdings was worse than any previous threat, eroding liberty and creating a barrier. The Taker's arsenal, comprised of ultra-destructive drakes and clickers, demolished infrastructure with dramatic attacks, creating a horizontal field of ruins. Nighthawk, with unweary commitment, watched the drakes jump and duckwalks through the garden, their keenness for destruction palpable. His internal button for justice was pressed. He knew he had to inherit the challenge. Through keyholes, he observed The Taker attempting to force a pliable cake onto a waiter, a morbid joke. Nighthawk knew his employment was now a matter of actuality, not just appearance. With the cunning of a decoy, Nighthawk handled the situation with professional tact, using a robust fabric suit that made him malleable to the onslaught. He fed the drakes nutritious nuggets, then used a fanny pack full of verbena to sedate them, turning the chaos into a jubilee. He watched the Taker, now timidly trying to dunker his teacup, realizing his license had been revoked. The survivor secured the trijets, ensuring no more attacks. His successful intervention prevented further endings, securing the city's future, nickel by nickel.
panelSentences: Nighthawk, a professional falcon and survivor, felt like a dorky reject in the grandiose central township. Despite his impeccable performance in system updates and maintaining healthy routines, newsrooms often overlooked him, spinning tales of impending endings. He tried to imbibe a sense of kinship but often felt like a blanket covering others. His turnkey equipment was always ready for any force threatening his universe. He dreamt of forbidden flights beyond the airlock, a shadow in his own billing.

Suddenly, a torrent of chaos was unleashed from the factories, attacking the city with bombastic force. A taker, a baboon-like figure, emerged from the shadow, gaffed from an ancient ontology update, brandishing a rickety tool. The entire system began to jammy, causing panic. Worry spread as people identified the suspect as 'The Kern Taker,' known for his habitual thwacks and jerky movements, leaving behind an icky trail. This monetary drain on holdings was worse than any previous threat, eroding liberty and creating a barrier.

The Taker's arsenal, comprised of ultra-destructive drakes and clickers, demolished infrastructure with dramatic attacks, creating a horizontal field of ruins. Nighthawk, with unweary commitment, watched the drakes jump and duckwalks through the garden, their keenness for destruction palpable. His internal button for justice was pressed. He knew he had to inherit the challenge. Through keyholes, he observed The Taker attempting to force a pliable cake onto a waiter, a morbid joke. Nighthawk knew his employment was now a matter of actuality, not just appearance.

With the cunning of a decoy, Nighthawk handled the situation with professional tact, using a robust fabric suit that made him malleable to the onslaught. He fed the drakes nutritious nuggets, then used a fanny pack full of verbena to sedate them, turning the chaos into a jubilee. He watched the Taker, now timidly trying to dunker his teacup, realizing his license had been revoked. The survivor secured the trijets, ensuring no more attacks. His successful intervention prevented further endings, securing the city's future, nickel by nickel.
rawPanelSentences: Nighthawk, a professional falcon and survivor, felt like a dorky reject in the grandiose central township. Despite his impeccable performance in system updates and maintaining healthy routines, newsrooms often overlooked him, spinning tales of impending endings. He tried to imbibe a sense of kinship but often felt like a blanket covering others. His turnkey equipment was always ready for any force threatening his universe. He dreamt of forbidden flights beyond the airlock, a shadow in his own billing.

Suddenly, a torrent of chaos was unleashed from the factories, attacking the city with bombastic force. A taker, a baboon-like figure, emerged from the shadow, gaffed from an ancient ontology update, brandishing a rickety tool. The entire system began to jammy, causing panic. Worry spread as people identified the suspect as 'The Kern Taker,' known for his habitual thwacks and jerky movements, leaving behind an icky trail. This monetary drain on holdings was worse than any previous threat, eroding liberty and creating a barrier.

The Taker's arsenal, comprised of ultra-destructive drakes and clickers, demolished infrastructure with dramatic attacks, creating a horizontal field of ruins. Nighthawk, with unweary commitment, watched the drakes jump and duckwalks through the garden, their keenness for destruction palpable. His internal button for justice was pressed. He knew he had to inherit the challenge. Through keyholes, he observed The Taker attempting to force a pliable cake onto a waiter, a morbid joke. Nighthawk knew his employment was now a matter of actuality, not just appearance.

With the cunning of a decoy, Nighthawk handled the situation with professional tact, using a robust fabric suit that made him malleable to the onslaught. He fed the drakes nutritious nuggets, then used a fanny pack full of verbena to sedate them, turning the chaos into a jubilee. He watched the Taker, now timidly trying to dunker his teacup, realizing his license had been revoked. The survivor secured the trijets, ensuring no more attacks. His successful intervention prevented further endings, securing the city's future, nickel by nickel.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist Nighthawk: wearing a matte black flight suit with glowing cyan accents, a silver falcon-shaped helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Nighthawk stands on a high-rise balcony, looking over a sprawling, futuristic city at dawn, a small airlock visible in the distance, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A giant, baboon-like figure, 'The Kern Taker,' emerges from a dilapidated factory, a torrent of glowing energy behind him, close-up, dynamic angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Nighthawk, crouched behind a collapsed, futuristic garden wall, observes several robotic 'drakes' demolishing furniture, eye-level shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Nighthawk, in a blur of action, offers a small glowing 'nugget' to a sedated drake while The Kern Taker sits dejectedly with a teacup, wide shot.

definitions.definition: Something that is rejected.
An unpopular person.
A rejected defective product in a production line
To refuse to accept.
definitions.example: She even rejected my improved offer.

definitions.definition: To block a shot, especially if it sends the ball off the court.
To refuse a romantic advance.
definitions.example: I've been rejected three times this week.

definitions.definition: A nightjar, especially Caprimulgus europaeus.
A New World nightjar of the genus Chordeiles, especially Chordeiles minor.
A person whose preference or custom is to remain awake and active during the night and the early morning hours.
A nighthawker.
Like a dork.
Enjoying health and vigor of body, mind, or spirit: well.
definitions.example: He was father to three healthy kids.

definitions.definition: Conducive to health.
definitions.example: A healthy diet and exercise can help to maintain proper weight.

definitions.definition: Evincing health.
Significant, hefty; beneficial.
definitions.example: Sam unwrapped the sandwich and took a healthy bite out of the middle.

definitions.definition: The act of performing; carrying into execution or action; execution; achievement; accomplishment; representation by action.
definitions.example: the performance of an undertaking or a duty

definitions.definition: That which is performed or accomplished; a thing done or carried through; an achievement; a deed; an act; a feat; especially, an action of an elaborate or public character.
A live show or concert.
The amount of useful work accomplished estimated in terms of time needed, resources used, etc.
definitions.example: Better performance means more work accomplished in shorter time and/or using fewer resources.

definitions.definition: The actual use of language in concrete situations by native speakers of a language, as opposed to the system of linguistic knowledge they possess (competence), cf. :w:linguistic performance.
A corn; grain; kernel.
The last handful or sheaf reaped at the harvest.
The harvest home.
One who takes something.
definitions.example: She is known as quite a risk taker.

definitions.definition: A person or thing that takes or receives, often more than he or she gives.
definitions.example: I don't want to be a relationship with you anymore - you are too much of a taker.

definitions.definition: One who is willing to participate in, or buy, something.
definitions.example: Are there any takers for helping me clean the garage this weekend?

definitions.definition: The territory of a town.
A subdivision of a county.
(Pre 1994) An area set aside for nonwhite occupation.
(Post 1994) A nonwhite (usually subeconomic) area attached to a city.
A small town.
A violent flow, as of water, lava, etc.; a stream suddenly raised and running rapidly, as down a precipice.
definitions.example: A torrent of green and white water broke over the hull of the sail-boat.

definitions.definition: A large amount or stream of something.
definitions.example: They endured a torrent of inquiries.

definitions.definition: Rolling or rushing in a rapid stream.
Perfect, without faults, flaws or errors
definitions.example: He grew up in Norway, but he writes impeccable English.

definitions.definition: Incapable of wrongdoing or sin; immaculate
Strength or energy of body or mind; active power; vigour; might; capacity of exercising an influence or producing an effect.
definitions.example: the force of an appeal, an argument, or a contract

definitions.definition: Power exerted against will or consent; compulsory power; violence; coercion.
Anything that is able to make a substantial change in a person or thing.

A physical quantity that denotes ability to push, pull, twist or accelerate a body and which has a direction and is measured in a unit dimensioned in mass × distance/time² (ML/T²): SI: newton (N); CGS: dyne (dyn)
Something or anything that has the power to produce a physical effect upon something else, such as causing it to move or change shape.
A group that aims to attack, control, or constrain.
definitions.example: police force

definitions.definition: The ability to attack, control, or constrain.
definitions.example: show of force

definitions.definition: A magic trick in which the outcome is known to the magician beforehand, especially one involving the apparent free choice of a card by another person.
Legal validity.
definitions.example: The law will come into force in January.

definitions.definition: Either unlawful violence, as in a "forced entry", or lawful compulsion.
Ability of an utterance or its element (word, form, prosody, ...) to effect a given meaning.
(with the, often capitalized) A metaphysical and ubiquitous power from the fictional Star Wars universe created by George Lucas. See usage note.
To violate (a woman); to rape.
To exert oneself, to do one's utmost.
To compel (someone or something) to do something.
To constrain by force; to overcome the limitations or resistance of.
To drive (something) by force, to propel (generally + prepositional phrase or adverb).
To cause to occur (despite inertia, resistance etc.); to produce through force.
definitions.example: The comedian's jokes weren't funny, but I forced a laugh now and then.

definitions.definition: To forcibly open (a door, lock etc.).
definitions.example: To force a lock.

definitions.definition: To obtain or win by strength; to take by violence or struggle; specifically, to capture by assault; to storm, as a fortress.
To create an out by touching a base in advance of a runner who has no base to return to while in possession of a ball which has already touched the ground.
definitions.example: Jones forced the runner at second by stepping on the bag.

definitions.definition: (whist) To compel (an adversary or partner) to trump a trick by leading a suit that he/she does not hold.
To put in force; to cause to be executed; to make binding; to enforce.
To provide with forces; to reinforce; to strengthen by soldiers; to man; to garrison.
To allow the force of; to value; to care for.
(law enforcement) Any police organization; a constabulary.
definitions.example: He joined the police force a long time ago, when he lived in Virginia.

definitions.definition: An Old World monkey of the genus Papio, having dog-like muzzles and large canine teeth, cheek pouches, a short tail, and naked callosities on the buttocks.
A foolish or boorish person.
A heavy, loosely woven fabric, usually large and woollen, used for warmth while sleeping or resting.
definitions.example: The baby was cold, so his mother put a blanket over him.

definitions.definition: A layer of anything.
definitions.example: The city woke under a thick blanket of fog.

definitions.definition: A thick rubber mat used in the offset printing process to transfer ink from the plate to the paper being printed.
definitions.example: A press operator must carefully wash the blanket whenever changing a plate.

definitions.definition: A streak or layer of blubber in whales.
To cover with, or as if with, a blanket.
definitions.example: A fresh layer of snow blanketed the area.

definitions.definition: To traverse or complete thoroughly.
definitions.example: The salesman blanketed the entire neighborhood.

definitions.definition: To toss in a blanket by way of punishment.
To take the wind out of the sails of (another vessel) by sailing to windward of it.
To nullify the impact of someone or something.
General; covering or encompassing everything.
definitions.example: They sought to create a blanket solution for all situations.

definitions.definition: The office of a news organisation, especially that part of it where the journalists work and news stories are processed.
A room where newspapers and magazines are available for reading.
To apply violent force to someone or something.
definitions.example: This species of snake will only attack humans if it feels threatened.

definitions.definition: To aggressively challenge a person, idea, etc., with words (particularly in newspaper headlines, because it typesets into less space than "criticize" or similar).
definitions.example: She published an article attacking the recent pay cuts.

definitions.definition: To begin to affect; to act upon injuriously or destructively; to begin to decompose or waste.
To deal with something in a direct way; to set to work upon.
definitions.example: I attacked the meal with a hearty appetite.

definitions.definition: To aim balls at the batsman’s wicket.
To set a field, or bowl in a manner designed to get wickets.
To bat aggressively, so as to score runs quickly.
To move forward in an active attempt to score a point, as opposed to trying not to concede.
To accelerate quickly in an attempt to get ahead of the other riders.
Carrying out an attack.
Verbena, a genus of herbaceous plants of which several species are extensively cultivated for the great beauty of their flowers; vervain.
Relation or connection by blood, marriage or adoption
Relation or connection by nature or character
To drink (used frequently of alcoholic beverages).
To take in; absorb.

definitions.definition: Any bird of the genus Falco, all of which are birds of prey.
A female such bird, a male being a tiercel.
A light cannon used from the 15th to the 17th century; a falconet.
To hunt with a falcon or falcons.
A collection of organized things; a whole composed of relationships among its members.

definitions.definition: A method or way of organizing or planning.
definitions.example: Followers should have a system to follow that works in their interests, not against them.

definitions.definition: A gun.
Resembling jam in taste, texture, etc.
Covered in jam.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Lucky, especially undeservedly so.
definitions.example: jammy bastard  jammy sod

definitions.definition: (Jewish history) A special year of emancipation supposed to be kept every fifty years, when farming was abandoned and Hebrew slaves were set free.
A 25th, 40th, 50th, 60th or 70th anniversary.
A special year (originally held every hundred years, then fifty, and then fewer) in which remission from sin could be granted as well as indulgences upon making a pilgrimage to Rome.
A time of celebration or rejoicing.
An occasion of mass manumission from slavery.
A period of fifty years; a half-century.
A termination or conclusion.
The last part of something.
(grammar) The last morpheme of a word, added to some base to make an inflected form (such as -ing in "ending").
The act of equipping, or the state of being equipped, as for a voyage or expedition.
Whatever is used in equipping something or someone, for example things needed for an expedition or voyage.
To take possession of as a right (especially in Biblical translations).

definitions.definition: To receive (property, a title, etc.), by legal succession or bequest after the previous owner's death.

definitions.definition: To receive a characteristic from one's ancestors by genetic transmission.
definitions.example: Let's hope the baby inherits his mother's looks and his father's intelligence.

definitions.definition: To derive from people or conditions previously in force.

definitions.definition: To come into an inheritance.

definitions.definition: To derive (existing functionality) from a superclass.

definitions.definition: To derive a new class from (a superclass).
To put in possession of.
Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.

definitions.definition: The sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: I think that the universe was created by a life force rather than a deity.

definitions.definition: An entity similar to our universe; one component of a larger entity known as the multiverse.
Everything under consideration.
definitions.example: In all this universe of possibilities, there is only one feasible option.

definitions.definition: A sample taken from the population.
An imaginary collection of worlds.

definitions.definition: (films) A collection of stories with characters and settings that are less interrelated than those of sequels or prequels.
A whole world, in the sense of perspective or social setting.

To cheat or hoax.
To gamble.
To affix or cover with gaffer tape.

A tidbit of something valuable.

definitions.definition: A small piece of tasty food, a tidbit.
A type of boot polish.
A bud from the Cannabis sativa plant, especially one that is potent.

A partial description gleaned from data mining.
The act of thwacking; a strike or blow, especially with a flat implement.
A heavy slapping sound.
To hit with a flat implement.
To beat.

The act of flying.

definitions.definition: An instance of flying.

definitions.definition: The act of fleeing.
(collective) A collective term for doves or swallows.

definitions.definition: A trip made by an aircraft, particularly one between two cities or countries, which is often planned or reserved in advance.
definitions.example: The flight to Paris leaves at 7 o'clock tonight.

definitions.definition: A series of stairs between landings.
A group of canal locks with a short distance between them
A floor which is reached by stairs or escalators.

definitions.definition: The feathers on an arrow or dart used to help it follow an even path.
A paper plane.
The movement of a spinning ball through the air - concerns its speed, trajectory and drift.
The ballistic trajectory of an arrow or other projectile.
An aerodynamic surface designed to guide such a projectile's trajectory.
An air force unit.
Several sample glasses of a specific wine varietal or other beverage. The pours are smaller than a full glass and the flight will generally include three to five different samples.
The shaped material forming the thread of a screw.
(of a spin bowler) To throw the ball in such a way that it has more airtime and more spin than usual.
(by extension) To throw or kick something so as to send it flying with more loft or airtime than usual.
Fast, swift, fleet.
To disallow; to proscribe.
definitions.example: Smoking in the restaurant is forbidden.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To deny, exclude from, or warn off, by express command.
To oppose, hinder, or prevent, as if by an effectual command.
definitions.example: An impassable river forbids the approach of the army.

definitions.definition: To accurse; to blast.
To defy; to challenge.
definitions.example: What part of "no" do you forbid to understand?

definitions.definition: Not allowed; specifically disallowed.
A dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object.
definitions.example: My shadow lengthened as the sun began to set.

definitions.definition: Relative darkness, especially as caused by the interruption of light; gloom, obscurity.
definitions.example: I immediately jumped into shadow as I saw them approach.

definitions.definition: A area protected by an obstacle (likened to an object blocking out sunlight).
definitions.example: The mountains block the passage of rain-producing weather systems and cast a "shadow" of dryness behind them.

definitions.definition: A reflected image, as in a mirror or in water.
That which looms as though a shadow.
definitions.example: I don't have a shadow of doubt in my mind that my plan will succeed. The shadow of fear of my being outed always affects how I live my life. I lived in her shadow my whole life.

definitions.definition: A small degree; a shade.
definitions.example: He did not give even a shadow of respect to the professor.

definitions.definition: An imperfect and faint representation.
definitions.example: He came back from war the shadow of a man.

definitions.definition: (police) A trainee, assigned to work with an experienced officer.
One who secretly or furtively follows another.
definitions.example: The constable was promoted to working as a shadow for the Royals.

definitions.definition: An inseparable companion.
A drop shadow effect applied to lettering in word processors etc.
An influence, especially a pervasive or a negative one.
A spirit; a ghost; a shade.
(Latinism) An uninvited guest accompanying one who was invited.
In Jungian psychology, an unconscious aspect of the personality.
To shade, cloud or darken.
definitions.example: The artist chose to shadow this corner of the painting.

definitions.definition: To block light or radio transmission from.
definitions.example: Looks like that cloud's going to shadow us.

definitions.definition: To secretly or discreetly track or follow another, to keep under surveillance.
To represent faintly and imperfectly.
To hide; to conceal.
To accompany (a professional) during the working day, so as to learn about an occupation one intends to take up.
To make (an identifier, usually a variable) inaccessible by declaring another of the same name within the scope of the first.
To apply the shadowing process to (the contents of ROM).
Unofficial, informal, unauthorized, but acting as though it were.
definitions.example: The human resources department has a shadow information technology group without headquarters knowledge.

definitions.definition: Having power or influence, but not widely known or recognized.
definitions.example: The director has been giving shadow leadership to the other group's project to ensure its success.

definitions.definition: Acting in a leadership role before being formally recognized.
definitions.example: The insurgents’ shadow government is being crippled by the federal military strikes.

definitions.definition: Part of, or related to, the opposition in government.
Of an object: not strong or sturdy, as because of poor construction or upkeep; not safe or secure.
definitions.example: He hesitated about climbing such a small, rickety ladder.

definitions.definition: Of a person: feeble in the joints; tottering.
definitions.example: The rickety old man hardly managed to climb the stairs.

definitions.definition: Affected with or suffering from rickets.
A noble or privileged social class, historically accompanied by a hereditary title; aristocracy.
The quality of being noble.
An esoteric doctrine or treatise; esoteric philosophy.
One who believes, or is an initiate, in esoteric doctrines or rites.
Intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of people with a specialized knowledge or interest, or an enlightened inner circle.
definitions.example: The writing in this manual is very esoteric; I’d need a degree in engineering just to understand it!

definitions.definition: Having to do with concepts that are highly theoretical and without obvious practical application; often with mystical or religious connotations.
Confidential; private.
definitions.example: an esoteric meeting

definitions.definition: A trading establishment, especially set up by merchants working in a foreign country.
The position or state of being a factor.
A building or other place where manufacturing takes place.
A device which produces or manufactures something.
A factory farm.

definitions.definition: In a computer program or library, a function, method, etc. which creates an object.
A police station.
Large and impressive, in size, scope or extent
Pompous or pretentious
An outdoor area containing one or more types of plants, usually plants grown for food or ornamental purposes.
definitions.example: a vegetable garden  a flower garden

definitions.definition: The grounds at the front or back of a house.
definitions.example: This house has a swimming pool, a tent, a swing set and a fountain in the garden.  We were drinking lemonade and playing croquet in the garden.  Our garden is overgrown with weeds.

definitions.definition: The twentieth Lenormand card.
A cluster; a bunch.
Pubic hair or the genitalia it masks.
definitions.example: N.B. From a commentary on Song of Solomon 4:16, which was written in Hebrew c. 950 BC; book footnotes are shown here within brackets. Many scholars disagree with this Biblical interpretation, which is included as evidence of the word's usage in 1995 rather than its intended meaning in 950 BC.

definitions.definition: To grow plants in a garden; to create or maintain a garden.
definitions.example: I love to garden — this year I'm going to plant some daffodils.

definitions.definition: Of a batsman, to inspect and tap the pitch lightly with the bat so as to smooth out small rough patches and irregularities.
Common, ordinary, domesticated.

A type of loaded walk in which the sportsman squats somewhat and steps forward or backward with his knees alternatingly while optionally carrying a dumbbell or kettlebell on each side or a kettlebell or cupped dumbbell between the legs.
A means of acceleration, moving with each foot turned 45 degrees from the forward position.

(preceded by definite article) A dance or dance move, popularised in the 1950s by Chuck Berry, in which the dancer steps forwards crouching on bended knees while keeping the back straight and head erect.
To jump on one leg while moving the other back and forth, a motion sometimes employed by guitar players in popular music.
To walk while squatting.
The act of judging.
The power or faculty of performing such operations; especially, when unqualified, the faculty of judging or deciding rightly, justly, or wisely
definitions.example: a man of judgment

definitions.definition: The conclusion or result of judging; an opinion; a decision.
The act of determining, as in courts of law, what is conformable to law and justice; also, the determination, decision, or sentence of a court, or of a judge.
The final award; the last sentence.
A person who belongs to a profession
A person who earns their living from a specified activity
A reputation known by name
An expert.
Of, pertaining to, or in accordance with the (usually high) standards of a profession.
That is carried out for money, especially as a livelihood.
(by extension) Expert.
The hole in a lock where the key is inserted and turns.
Any small opening resembling the hole for a key in shape or function.
A circle cut out of a garment as a decorative effect, typically at the front or back neckline of a dress.
A hole or excavation in beams intended to be joined together, to receive the key that fastens them.
A mortise for a key or cotter.
(lasers) A transient column of vapor or plasma formed when using high energy beams, such as lasers, for welding or cutting.
definitions.example: keyhole plasma

definitions.definition: A welding method in which a hole forms in the surface immediately ahead of the puddle in the direction of welding. The hole is filled as the weld progresses.
The free-throw lane together with the circle surrounding the free-throw line; key.
A gravitational keyhole.

(ordnance) To strike a target after wobbling in flight so that the long axis of the bullet does not follow the line of flight; typically due to insufficient spin resulting from the rifling in the barrel.
Large movable item(s), usually in a room, which enhance(s) the room's characteristics, functionally or decoratively.
definitions.example: A chair is furniture. Sofas are also furniture.

definitions.definition: The harness, trappings etc. of a horse, hawk, or other animal.
Fittings, such as handles, of a door, coffin, or other wooden item.
The stock and forearm of a weapon.
The pieces of wood or metal put round pages of type to make proper margins and fill the spaces between the pages and the chase.
Any material on the page other than the text and pictures of stories.
The remote-control device used to change settings on a television set, VCR, or other electronic equipment.
definitions.example: We have a clicker for the TV, one for the VCR, one for the DVD player and another one that does it all.

definitions.definition: An electronic device used by individual students in the classroom to respond to multiple-choice questions, etc.
A person who cuts out the uppers of shoes from pieces of leather using a flexible knife that clicks as it changes direction.
A machine that cuts materials using a steel rule die. The name comes from the sound (click) when the material is cut. May be hand, pneumatic, or hydraulic powered.
A signalling device used by military forces. Pressed between thumb and fingers, it makes a small but distinctive click understood by other members of a unit.
A small mechanical device that produces a clicking sound, used in dog training.
Someone who clicks, for example using a computer mouse.
One who stands before a shop door to invite people to buy.
One who has charge of the work of a companionship.
An employee who locks the type in the form to make it ready for printing.
A small amount of air in a pipe that stops liquid flowing through it
A sealed, airtight chamber, such as in a manned spacecraft or submarine, used to provide access to and from the sealed area without allowing air out or water in.
The act of jumping; a leap; a spring; a bound.
An effort; an attempt; a venture.
A dislocation in a stratum; a fault.
An abrupt interruption of level in a piece of brickwork or masonry.
An instance of propelling oneself upwards.
definitions.example: The boy took a skip and a jump down the lane.

definitions.definition: An object which causes one to jump, a ramp.
definitions.example: He went off a jump.

definitions.definition: An instance of causing oneself to fall from an elevated location.
definitions.example: There were a couple of jumps from the bridge.

definitions.definition: An instance of employing a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
definitions.example: She was terrified before the jump, but was thrilled to be skydiving.

definitions.definition: An instance of reacting to a sudden stimulus by jerking the body.
A jumping move in a board game.
definitions.example: the knight's jump in chess

definitions.definition: A button (of a joypad, joystick or similar device) used to make a video game character jump (propel itself upwards).
definitions.example: Press jump to start.

definitions.definition: An obstacle that forms part of a showjumping course, and that the horse has to jump over cleanly.
definitions.example: Heartless managed the scale the first jump but fell over the second.

definitions.definition: (with on) An early start or an advantage.
definitions.example: He got a jump on the day because he had laid out everything the night before.

definitions.definition: A discontinuity in the graph of a function, where the function is continuous in a punctured interval of the discontinuity.
An instance of faster-than-light travel, not observable from ordinary space.
A change of the path of execution to a different location.
Short for jump-start.
definitions.example: My car won't start. Could you give me a jump?

definitions.definition: To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
Exact; matched; fitting; precise.
Exactly; precisely
An occasion when a performer or team of them (especially in vaudeville) expects to perform at a theater for a single evening.

A single sexual encounter between two individuals, where at least one of the partners has no immediate intention or expectation of establishing a longer-term sexual or romantic relationship. As the phrase implies, the relationship lasts for only one night.
Either of the two partners involved in such a single sexual encounter.
Able to be hammered into thin sheets; capable of being extended or shaped by beating with a hammer, or by the pressure of rollers.
Flexible, liable to change.
definitions.example: My opinion on the subject is malleable.

definitions.definition: (of an algorithm) in which an adversary can alter a ciphertext such that it decrypts to a related plaintext
A male or female attendant who serves customers at their tables in a restaurant, café or similar.
definitions.example: Waiter! There's a fly in my soup.

definitions.definition: Someone who waits for somebody or something; a person who is waiting.
A person working as an attendant at the London Stock Exchange.
A vessel or tray on which something is carried, as dishes, etc.; a salver. (See etymology of dumbwaiter.)
A custom house officer; a tide waiter.
A watchman.
The whole of something; the entirety.
An uncastrated horse; a stallion.
A complete envelope with stamps and all official markings: (prior to the use of envelopes) a page folded and posted.
Porter or stout as delivered from the brewery.
(sometimes postpositive) Whole; complete.
definitions.example: We had the entire building to ourselves for the evening.

definitions.definition: Having a smooth margin without any indentation.
Consisting of a single piece, as a corolla.
(of a complex function) Complex-differentiable on all of ℂ.
(of a male animal) Not gelded.
Morally whole; pure; sheer
Internal; interior.
Unpleasantly sticky; yucky; disgusting.
definitions.example: I stepped in something icky and it smells terrible.

definitions.definition: Excessively sentimental.
Unwell or upset; in a bad state of mind or health.
definitions.example: I'm not going to work today. I just feel icky.

definitions.definition: A rich, sweet dessert food, typically made of flour, sugar and eggs and baked in an oven, and often covered in icing.
A small mass of baked dough, especially a thin loaf from unleavened dough.
definitions.example: a johnnycake

definitions.definition: A thin wafer-shaped mass of fried batter; a griddlecake or pancake.
definitions.example: buckwheat cakes

definitions.definition: A block of any of various dense materials.
definitions.example: a cake of sand

definitions.definition: A trivially easy task or responsibility; from a piece of cake.
Money.
Used to describe the doctrine of having one's cake and eating it too.
A buttock, especially one that is exceptionally plump.
definitions.example: Mmm, I'd like to cut me some of that cake!

definitions.definition: Coat (something) with a crust of solid material.
definitions.example: His shoes are caked with mud.

definitions.definition: To form into a cake, or mass.
A person who is suspected of something, in particular of committing a crime.
To imagine or suppose (something) to be true, or to exist, without proof.
definitions.example: to suspect the presence of disease

definitions.definition: To distrust or have doubts about (something or someone).
definitions.example: to suspect the truth of a story

definitions.definition: To believe (someone) to be guilty.
definitions.example: I suspect him of being the thief.

definitions.definition: To have suspicion.
To look up to; to respect.
Viewed with suspicion; suspected.
Viewing with suspicion; suspecting.
(of a person, their language or writing) showy in speech and given to using flowery or elaborate terms; grandiloquent; pompous
High-sounding but with little meaning.
Inflated, overfilled.
A male duck.
A mayfly used as fishing bait.
A dragon.
A small piece of artillery.
A fiery meteor.
A beaked galley, or Viking warship.
One who does something habitually, such as a serial criminal offender.
(grammar) A construction representing something done habitually.
Of or relating to a habit; established as a habit; performed over and over again; recurrent, recurring.
definitions.example: Her habitual lying was the reason for my mistrust.

definitions.definition: Regular or usual.
definitions.example: Professor Franklein took his habitual seat at the conference table.

definitions.definition: Of a person or thing: engaging in some behaviour as a habit or regularly.
definitions.example: He’s a habitual chain-smoker.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Pertaining to an action performed customarily, ordinarily, or usually.
To toss up the head frequently, as a horse to avoid the restraint of the bridle.

(ice-skating) To not broadcast a medal-winning or otherwise memorable or crucial figure skating performance. This only occurs in a live broadcast because the network has to decide which programs to show and which to cut in the interest of time. If a skater is low in the rankings and several big names are set to skate later, that performance may be cut.
Of birds: to make a sudden harsh call.
The unordered state of matter in classical accounts of cosmogony.
Any state of disorder; a confused or amorphous mixture or conglomeration.
definitions.example: After the earthquake, the local hospital was in chaos

definitions.definition: A behaviour of iterative non-linear systems in which arbitrarily small variations in initial conditions become magnified over time.
One of the two metaphysical forces of the world in some fantasy settings, as opposed to law.
A vast chasm or abyss.
A given medium; a space in which something exists or lives; an environment.
One who adjusts, especially for the insurance industry's employment title "loss adjuster" (or "claims adjuster" in the United States).
A knob or disc that is passed through a loop or (buttonhole), serving as a fastener.
definitions.example: April fastened the buttons of her overcoat to keep out the wind.

definitions.definition: A mechanical device meant to be pressed with a finger in order to open or close an electric circuit or to activate a mechanism.
definitions.example: Pat pushed the button marked "shred" on the blender.

definitions.definition: An on-screen control that can be selected as an activator of an attached function.
definitions.example: Click the button that looks like a house to return to your browser's home page.

definitions.definition: A badge worn on clothes, fixed with a pin through the fabric.
definitions.example: The politician wore a bright yellow button with the slogan "Vote Smart" emblazoned on it.

definitions.definition: A bud.
The head of an unexpanded mushroom.
The clitoris.
The center (bullseye) of the house.
The soft circular tip at the end of a foil.
A plastic disk used to represent the person in last position in a poker game; also dealer's button.
The player who is last to act after the flop, turn and river, who possesses the button.
A person who acts as a decoy.
A raised pavement marker to further indicate the presence of a pavement marking painted stripe.
A methaqualone tablet (used as a recreational drug).
A piece of wood or metal, usually flat and elongated, turning on a nail or screw, to fasten something, such as a door.
A globule of metal remaining on an assay cupel or in a crucible, after fusion.
A knob; a small ball; a small, roundish mass.
A small white blotch on a cat's coat.
A unit of length equal to 1/12 inch.
The means for initiating a nuclear strike or similar cataclysmic occurrence.
In an instrument of the violin family, the near-semicircular shape extending from the top of the back plate of the instrument, meeting the heel of the neck.
The least amount of care or interest; a whit or jot.
The final joke at the end of a comedic act (such as a sketch, set, or scene).
A button man; a professional assassin.
The final segment of a rattlesnake's rattle.
In a violin-family instrument, the carved wooden plug which sits in the bottom block of the instrument.
The condition of being free from control or restrictions.
definitions.example: The army is here, your liberty is assured.

definitions.definition: The condition of being free from imprisonment, slavery or forced labour.
definitions.example: The prisoners gained their liberty from an underground tunnel.

definitions.definition: The condition of being free to act, believe or express oneself as one chooses.
definitions.example: Everyone has the right to life, liberty and security of person.

definitions.definition: Freedom from excessive government control.
A short period when a sailor is allowed ashore.
definitions.example: We're going on a three-day liberty as soon as we dock.

definitions.definition: (often plural) A breach of social convention.
definitions.example: You needn't take such liberties.

definitions.definition: A local division of government administration in medieval England.
(game of go) an empty space next to a group of stones of the same color.
A legal document giving official permission to do something; a permit.
The legal terms under which a person is allowed to use a product, especially software.
Freedom to deviate deliberately from normally applicable rules or practices (especially in behaviour or speech).
Excessive freedom; lack of due restraint.
Short for driver's license.
definitions.example: In order to enter the building, I need to show my license.

definitions.definition: To give a formal (usually written) authorization.
definitions.example: It was decided to license Wikipedia under the GFDL.

definitions.definition: Authorize officially.
definitions.example: I am licensed to practice law in this state.

definitions.definition: A person or object meant to lure somebody into danger.
A real or fake animal used by hunters to lure game.
To lead into danger by artifice; to lure into a net or snare; to entrap.
definitions.example: to decoy troops into an ambush; to decoy ducks into a net

definitions.definition: To act as, or use, a decoy.
To destroy.
definitions.example: They demolished the old mill and put up four townhouses.

definitions.definition: To defeat or consume utterly (as a theory, belief or opponent).
Characterized by physical jerking.
Having the behavior of a jerk (unpleasant person).
Thinking about unpleasant things that have happened or that might happen; feeling afraid and unhappy.
definitions.example: She was worried about her son who had been sent off to fight in the war.

definitions.definition: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.
definitions.example: Stop worrying about your test, it’ll be fine.

definitions.definition: Disturb the peace of mind of; afflict with mental agitation or distress.
definitions.example: Your tone of voice worries me.

definitions.definition: To harass; to irritate or distress.
definitions.example: The President was worried into military action by persistent advisors.

definitions.definition: To seize or shake by the throat, especially of a dog or wolf.
definitions.example: Your dog’s been worrying sheep again.

definitions.definition: To touch repeatedly, to fiddle with.
To strangle.
To establish the identity of someone or something.
To disclose the identity of someone.
definitions.example: The Associated Press will not identify the suspect of the crime because he is a juvenile.

definitions.definition: To establish the taxonomic classification of an organism.
To equate or make the same; to unite or combine into one.
To have a strong affinity with; to feel oneself to be modelled on or connected to.
To associate oneself with some group.
To claim an identity; to describe oneself as a member of a group; to assert the use of a particular term to describe oneself.
To drive oakum into the seams of a ship's wooden deck or hull to make it watertight.
To apply caulking to joints, cracks, or a juncture of different materials.
Fuck
Having cracks or crevices stopped-up with a filler.
The branch of metaphysics that addresses the nature or essential characteristics of being and of things that exist; the study of being qua being.

In a subject view, or a world view, the set of conceptual or material things or classes of things that are recognised as existing, or are assumed to exist in context; in a body of theory, the ontology comprises the domain of discourse, the things that are defined as existing, together with whatever emerges from their mutual implications.
The theory of a particular philosopher or school of thought concerning the fundamental types of entity in the universe.
A logical system involving theory of classes, developed by Stanislaw Lesniewski (1886-1939).
A structure of concepts or entities within a domain, organized by relationships; a system model.
An advisement providing more up-to-date information than currently known.
definitions.example: He gave me an update on the situation in New York.

definitions.definition: A change in information, a modification of existing or known data.
definitions.example: I just made an update to the Wikipedia article on guerillas.

definitions.definition: An additional piece of information. An addition to existing information.
definitions.example: I just made an update to my blog about my trip to Rome.

definitions.definition: A modification of something to a more recent, up-to-date version; (in software) a minor upgrade.
definitions.example: I have a couple of updates to install on your laptop.

definitions.definition: A version of something which is newer than other versions.
definitions.example: You should try the update: it rocks.

definitions.definition: To bring (a thing) up to date.
definitions.example: I need to update my records to take account of the most recent transaction.

definitions.definition: To bring (a person) up to date: to inform (a person) about recent developments.
definitions.example: Update me on what happened while I was away.

definitions.definition: To refresh (a person) after weariness.
Not weary.
An ultraroyalist in France.
An extremist, especially an ultranationalist.
An especially devoted football fan, typically associated with the intimidating use of extremist slogans, pyrotechnics and sometimes hooligan violence.
An ultramarathon.
An ultra-prominent peak.
(usually capitalised) Code name used by British codebreakers during World War 2 for decrypted information gained from the enemy.
Extreme; far beyond the norm; fanatical; uncompromising.
definitions.example: an ultra reformer; ultra measures

definitions.definition: (of food or drink) Providing nutrients; healthy to eat.
Someone who dunks.
A person tasked with performing or training others in slam dunks.
A biscuit that is suitable for dunking in a cup of tea.
Any snack food suitable for dunking in sauce.
A kind of sonobuoy.
A mound, pile, or stack of stored hay.
(canoeing) A standing wave in a rapid.
The text string within which another string is searched for. (see: needle in a haystack)
A dish composed of a starchy food (rice, tortillas, crackers, etc.) topped by a protein (beans, cheese, meat, etc.) in combination with fresh vegetables, assembled on the plate by the diner.
Of, pertaining to, or consisting of money.
Unfavorable; negative; not good.
definitions.example: Abducting children is bad for mothers.

definitions.definition: Not suitable or fitting.
definitions.example: Do you think it is a bad idea to confront him directly?

definitions.definition: Not appropriate, of manners etc.
definitions.example: It is bad manners to talk with your mouth full.

definitions.definition: Unhealthy; liable to cause health problems.
definitions.example: Lard is bad for you. Smoking is bad for you, too. Grapes are bad for dogs but not for humans.

definitions.definition: Sickly, unhealthy, unwell.
definitions.example: I went to the hospital to see how my grandfather was doing. Unfortunately, he's in a bad state.

definitions.definition: Not behaving; behaving badly; misbehaving; mischievous or disobedient.
definitions.example: Stop being bad, or you will get a spanking!

definitions.definition: Tricky; stressful; unpleasant.
definitions.example: Divorce is usually a bad experience for everybody involved.

definitions.definition: Evil; wicked.
definitions.example: Be careful. There are bad people in the world.

definitions.definition: Faulty; not functional.
definitions.example: I had a bad headlight.

definitions.definition: (of food) Spoiled, rotten, overripe.
definitions.example: These apples have gone bad.

definitions.definition: (of breath) Malodorous; foul.
definitions.example: Bad breath is not pleasant for anyone.

definitions.definition: False; counterfeit; illegitimate.
definitions.example: They were caught trying to pass bad coinage.

definitions.definition: Unskilled; of limited ability; not good.

definitions.definition: Of poor physical appearance.
definitions.example: I don't look bad in this dress, do I?

definitions.definition: Bold and daring.
Good, superlative, excellent, cool.
definitions.example: Man, that new car you bought is bad!

definitions.definition: (of a need, want, or pain) Severe, urgent.
definitions.example: He is in bad need of a haircut.

definitions.definition: Overly promiscuous, licentious.
(of a draft/check) Not covered by funds on account.
definitions.example: He gave me a bad check.

definitions.definition: In a bad manner.
Very much; to a great degree.
definitions.example: I want it so badly.

definitions.definition: Evil; wicked (of people).
Morally reprehensible (of behaviour etc.); blameworthy.
Indicative of unkind or malevolent intentions; harsh, cruel.
definitions.example: He suffered from ill treatment.

definitions.definition: Unpropitious, unkind, faulty, not up to reasonable standard.
definitions.example: ill manners; ill will

definitions.definition: Unwell in terms of health or physical condition; sick.
definitions.example: I've been ill with the flu for the past few days.

definitions.definition: Having an urge to vomit.
definitions.example: Seeing those pictures made me ill.

definitions.definition: (hip-hop slang) Sublime, with the connotation of being so in a singularly creative way.
Extremely bad (bad enough to make one ill). Generally used indirectly with to be.
definitions.example: That band was ill.

definitions.definition: Unwise; not a good idea.
Not well; imperfectly, badly; hardly.
Loss; disadvantage; defeat.
That which is worse; something less good.
definitions.example: Do not think the worse of him for his enterprise.

definitions.definition: To make worse; to put at disadvantage; to discomfit.
An aircraft powered by three jet engines.
Three jets of particles from an event or source
An edifice or building.
The act of constructing, construction, fabrication.
The structure of anything, the manner in which the parts of a thing are united; workmanship, texture, make.
definitions.example: cloth of a beautiful fabric

definitions.definition: The framework underlying a structure.
definitions.example: the fabric of our lives

definitions.definition: A material made of fibers, a textile or cloth.
definitions.example: cotton fabric

definitions.definition: The appearance of crystalline grains in a rock.
Interconnected nodes that look like a textile fabric when diagrammed.
definitions.example: The Internet is a fabric of computers connected by routers.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Fastidious and fussy; difficult to please; exacting, especially about details.
definitions.example: My editor is very finicky about punctuation. Every dot and comma has to be just right.

definitions.definition: Demanding; requiring above-normal care.
definitions.example: The lawnmower is a bit finicky in cold weather.

definitions.definition: Resulting in success; assuring, or promoting, success; accomplishing what was proposed; having the desired effect
definitions.example: a successful use of medicine;  a successful experiment;  a successful enterprise

definitions.definition: One who survives, especially one who survives a traumatic experience.
definitions.example: From this moment on, I choose to live not as a victim, but as a survivor.

definitions.definition: A person who is able to endure hardship.
One who knew a specific decedent.
definitions.example: She was from a large family and had many friends, so the funeral was crowded with mourning survivors.

definitions.definition: A silvery elemental metal with an atomic number of 28 and symbol Ni.
A coin worth 5 cents.
(by extension) Five dollars.
(by extension) Five hundred dollars.
(sometimes the nickel or the hot nickel) Interstate 5, a highway that runs along the west coast of the United States.
A playing card with the rank of five
A five-year prison sentence.
A defensive formation with five defensive backs, one of whom is a nickelback, instead of the more common four.
To plate with nickel.
An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
An attempt to detract from the worth or credibility of, a person, position, idea, object, or thing, by physical, verbal, emotional, or other assault.
definitions.example: They claimed the censorship of the article was an attack on free speech.

definitions.definition: A time in which one attacks; the offence of a battle.
definitions.example: The army timed their attack to coincide with the local celebrations.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The beginning of active operations on anything.
definitions.example: Having washed the plates from dinner, I made an attack on the laundry.

definitions.definition: An attempt to exploit a vulnerability in a computer system.
Collectively, the bowlers of a cricket side.
Any contact with the ball other than a serve or block which sends the ball across the plane of the net.
The three attackmen on the field or all the attackmen of a team.
The sudden onset of a disease or condition.
definitions.example: I've had an attack of the flu.

definitions.definition: An active episode of a chronic or recurrent disease.
The onset of a musical note, particularly with respect to the strength (and duration) of that onset.

(audio) The amount of time it takes for the volume of an audio signal to go from zero to maximum level (e.g. an audio waveform representing a snare drum hit would feature a very fast attack, whereas that of a wave washing to shore would feature a slow attack).
To apply violent force to someone or something.
definitions.example: This species of snake will only attack humans if it feels threatened.

definitions.definition: To aggressively challenge a person, idea, etc., with words (particularly in newspaper headlines, because it typesets into less space than "criticize" or similar).
definitions.example: She published an article attacking the recent pay cuts.

definitions.definition: To begin to affect; to act upon injuriously or destructively; to begin to decompose or waste.
To deal with something in a direct way; to set to work upon.

definitions.definition: To aim balls at the batsman’s wicket.
To set a field, or bowl in a manner designed to get wickets.
To bat aggressively, so as to score runs quickly.
To move forward in an active attempt to score a point, as opposed to trying not to concede.
To accelerate quickly in an attempt to get ahead of the other riders.
A military establishment for the storing, development, manufacturing, testing, or repairing of arms, ammunition, and other war materiel; an armoury.
A stock of weapons, especially all the weapons that a nation possesses.
A store or supply of anything.
Any supply of aid collected to prepare a person or army for hardship

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the drama.
Striking in appearance or effect.
Having a powerful, expressive singing voice.
Something that one owns, especially stocks and bonds.
A determination of law made by a court.
A tenure; a farm or other estate held of another.
That which holds, binds, or influences.
Logic; consistency.
The burden or chorus of a song.

A horizontal component of a structure
Horizon

Perpendicular to the vertical; parallel to the plane of the horizon; level, flat
Relating to horizontal markets
Pertaining to the horizon

definitions.definition: (wine tasting) involving wines of the same vintages but from different wineries
Of an interval: having the two notes sound successively.
Sharpness or cutting ability
Astuteness or sagacity
Eagerness or enthusiasm
A warder or jailer/gaoler; keeper of the keys in a prison.
To supply a turnkey product; to supply something fully assembled and ready to use

definitions.definition: A small cup, usually with a handle, used for drinking tea; normally sits in a saucer as part of a tea set.
A unit of measure; a teacupful.
Of an animal, smaller than average.
The female genitalia.

definitions.definition: The buttocks; arguably the most nearly polite of several euphemisms.

definitions.definition: Women viewed as sexual objects.

The trait of sincerity and focused purpose.
Perpetration as in a crime or mistake.
State of being pledged or engaged.

A structure that bars passage.
An obstacle or impediment.
A boundary or limit.

The state of existing; existence.

Live reporting on current affairs.
A short early motion picture.
The act of appearing or coming into sight; the act of becoming visible to the eye.
definitions.example: His sudden appearance surprised me.

definitions.definition: A thing seen; a phenomenon; an apparition.
definitions.example: There was a strange appearance in the sky.

definitions.definition: The way something looks; personal presence
Apparent likeness; the way which something or someone appears to others.
definitions.example: Some people say I'm shallow because I care so much about my appearance.

definitions.definition: That which is not substance, essence, hypostasis; the outward reality as opposed to the underlying reality
definitions.example: Catholicism teaches that the Eucharist, while remaining under the physical appearance of bread and wine, becomes really and truly the body and the blood of Christ.

definitions.definition: The act of appearing in a particular place, or in society, a company, or any proceedings; a coming before the public in a particular character.
definitions.example: A person makes his appearance as an historian, an artist, or an orator.

definitions.definition: An instance of someone coming into a court of law to be part of a trial, either in person or represented by an attorney or such like; a court appearance
Chiefly used by nurses: the act of defecation by a patient.
definitions.example: The patient had a small bowel obstruction and there was no appearance until after the obstruction resolved.

definitions.definition: To touch; to feel or hold with the hand(s).
To accustom to the hand; to take care of with the hands.
To manage, use, or wield with the hands.
To manage, control, or direct.
To treat, to deal with (in a specified way).
definitions.example: she handled the news with grace, the Persians handled the French ambassador shamefully

definitions.definition: To deal with (a subject, argument, topic, or theme) in speaking, in writing, or in art.
To receive and transfer; to have pass through one's hands; hence, to buy and sell.
definitions.example: a merchant handles a variety of goods, or a large stock

definitions.definition: To be concerned with; to be an expert in.
To put up with; to endure (and continue to function).
definitions.example: I can't handle this hot weather.

definitions.definition: To use the hands.
To illegally touch the ball with the hand or arm; to commit handball.
To behave in a particular way when handled (managed, controlled, directed).
definitions.example: the car handles well

definitions.definition: Having a specified number or kind of handles.
definitions.example: a two-handled drinking cup

definitions.definition: A rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town.
definitions.example: There are 2 churches and 3 shops in our village.

definitions.definition: A rural habitation that has a church, but no market.
A planned community such as a retirement community or shopping district.
A gated community.
A mechanical device intended to make a task easier.
definitions.example: Hand me that tool, would you?   I don't have the right tools to start fiddling around with the engine.

definitions.definition: Equipment used in a profession, e.g., tools of the trade.
definitions.example: These are the tools of the trade.

definitions.definition: Something to perform an operation; an instrument; a means.
A piece of software used to develop software or hardware, or to perform low-level operations.
definitions.example: The software engineer had been developing lots of EDA tools.   a tool for recovering deleted files from a disk

definitions.definition: A person or group which is used or controlled, usually unwittingly, by another person or group.
definitions.example: He was a tool, no more than a pawn to her.

definitions.definition: Penis.
(by extension) An obnoxious or uptight person.
definitions.example: He won't sell us tickets because it's 3:01, and they went off sale at 3. That guy's such a tool.

definitions.definition: To work on or shape with tools, e.g., hand-tooled leather.
To equip with tools.
To work very hard.
To put down another person (possibly in a subtle, hidden way), and in that way to use him or her to meet a goal.
definitions.example: Dude, he's not your friend. He's just tooling you.

definitions.definition: To intentionally attack the ball so that it deflects off a blocker out of bounds.
To drive (a coach or other vehicle).
To carry or convey in a coach or other vehicle.
To travel in a vehicle; to ride or drive.
In a timid manner.
The work or occupation for which one is used, and often paid
The act of employing
A use, purpose
definitions.example: The personnel director handled the whole employment procedure

definitions.definition: The state of being employed
An activity to which one devotes time
The number or percentage of people at work
